title: papaver radicatum f. normale
intro: Papaver radicatum f. normale, also known as the Alpine poppy, is a plant species within the Papaver genus, characterized by its bright orange-red flowers and unique adaptations to high-altitude environments. The plant contains various alkaloids, including sanguinarine and dihydrochelerythrine, which exhibit medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Papaver radicatum f. normale include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although further research is needed to confirm these effects. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
